navigation_overview: Parabank is a sample banking application. When visitors arrive, they see the sign-in page, which offers an Email / Username and Password form, a Register link for new customers, and a Forgot Password? link for credential recovery. After a successful log-in, users reach their account dashboard displaying every account and balance. The left-hand navigation menu now contains the following options: ① Accounts Overview (default dashboard view), ② Open New Account, ③ Transfer Funds, ④ Bill Pay, ⑤ Request Loan, ⑥ Update Contact Info, ⑦ Manage Cards, ⑧ Investments, ⑨ Account Statements, ⑩ Security Settings, ⑪ Support Center, and ⑫ Log Out.

module_summaries.summary:
Users can sign in using Email/Username and Password or use the Forgot Password link.
Shows the login form fields, validation feedback, and authentication success/failure messages.

Users can create a new account by completing the registration form and submitting Register.
Shows required form fields (name, address, SSN, phone, username/email, password, ZIP, state) plus inline validation feedback.

Users can view their list of customer accounts and select account rows for further actions.
Shows a welcome message and a table of accounts with masked Account Number, Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status, Open Date and a total-balance footer.

Users can open a new Checking or Savings account by selecting an account type, entering an initial deposit and choosing a funding source, then submit Open Account.
Shows account type selection cards with features and minimums, Initial Deposit field, Funding Source dropdown and real-time validation/error messages.

Users can initiate transfers between their ParaBank accounts or to external accounts by selecting transfer type, source, destination and amount, then submit the transfer.
Shows transfer type options, Source Account dropdown (Checking/Savings), destination inputs that change for internal vs external transfers (internal account selector or external account + confirm) and validation/errors.

Users can schedule and submit bill payments by entering payee details, payee account numbers, payment amount and selecting a source account, then submit Pay.
Shows payee name and address fields, phone, payee account and confirm fields, Payment Amount, Source Account dropdown, submission result with reference code or error messages.

Users can apply for Personal, Auto, or Home loans by choosing a loan type, entering loan and down payment amounts and selecting a collateral account, then submit the application.
Shows loan type cards with interest rates, Loan Amount field (type-specific ranges), Down Payment, Collateral Account dropdown and approval/validation feedback.

Users can edit and save their contact/profile information using the Update Profile action.
Shows a pre-filled editable form (First Name, Last Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, Phone Number), inline validation errors and a success or error banner.

Users can request a new debit or credit card and manage existing cards by setting spending limits, travel notices or changing card status.
Shows two stacked forms: card request fields (Card Type, Account to Link, Shipping Address) and card controls (Select Existing Card, New Spending Limit, Travel Notice, Card Status) plus submission results and tracking IDs.

Users can review their investment portfolio and execute Buy or Sell fund trades by selecting action, fund symbol, quantity and funding/destination account then Execute Trade.
Shows a portfolio snapshot (holdings, market value, unrealised gain/loss), trade form (Action, Fund Symbol autocomplete, Quantity, Funding/Destination Account) and trade validation/results.

Users can generate account statements for a chosen period and account and manage e-statement (paperless) preferences.
Shows statement generation controls (month/year or custom date range, Account), retrieved transactions on success, and e-statement preference controls (checkbox, Email Address) with save status.

Users can change their password by entering Current Password, New Password and Confirm New Password then submit Change Password.
Shows the change-password form inside a collapsible panel with fields for Current, New and Confirm New Password and validation/error feedback.

Users can send secure messages (with optional attachments) or request a callback by completing the respective forms and submitting.
Shows the message form (Subject, Category, Message Body, Attachment) and the callback form (Reason, Preferred Date, Preferred Time Window, Phone Number) plus ticket/confirmation info and inline errors.

test_cases.title: Protected routes inaccessible after logout
test_cases.preconditions: User has just logged out after an active session
steps: After completing logout, attempt to navigate to a protected page such as Open New Account by entering its URL or using the navigation.
Observe whether the page loads or access is blocked.
test_cases.expected_result: Access to the protected page is denied or the user is redirected to the Login page, preventing access after logout.

test_cases.title: Direct URL access while logged out redirects to login
test_cases.preconditions: User is not logged in
steps: Ensure the browser session is logged out (use private/incognito or sign out).
Enter the direct URL for the Accounts Overview page in the address bar and navigate to it.
Observe the browser's resulting page.
test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected to the Login page and does not see the protected Accounts Overview content.

test_cases.title: Logout from user menu terminates session and shows login page
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in
steps: Open the user/profile menu in the application while logged in.
Click the Log Out option from the menu.
Observe the resulting page and visible UI elements.
test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected to the Login page and authenticated content is no longer visible, indicating the session was terminated.

test_cases.title: Sign in with password exactly at minimum length meeting complexity
test_cases.preconditions: A registered user exists whose password is exactly the minimum allowed length and meets complexity
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username with valid identifier, Password with an exactly minimum-length password that meets complexity)
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Flash "Signed in successfully." is shown and the user is redirected to the Accounts Overview page

test_cases.title: Browser refresh after logout does not restore authenticated session
test_cases.preconditions: User has just logged out
steps: Immediately after logging out, refresh the browser page.
Observe whether authenticated content reappears or the Login page remains.
test_cases.expected_result: Refreshing the browser does not restore the authenticated session; the Login page remains and authenticated content does not reappear.

test_cases.title: Attempt sign in with incorrect credentials
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username with a valid identifier, Password with an incorrect password for that account)
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Error message is displayed, the password field is cleared, and another attempt is allowed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Incorrect email or password. Please try again,

test_cases.title: Page refresh while logged in keeps user logged in
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in on an authenticated page
steps: While logged in and viewing the Accounts Overview page, note visible authenticated content (account list/balances).
Refresh the browser page.
Verify the page reloads and the authenticated content remains visible without redirecting to login.
test_cases.expected_result: The page reloads without redirecting to the Login page and authenticated content remains visible.

test_cases.title: Already-logged-in user navigating to login URL is redirected to authenticated landing page
test_cases.preconditions: User is already logged in
steps: While logged in, navigate to the application's Login page URL.
Observe whether the application allows viewing the login form or redirects.
Confirm the user is redirected to the authenticated landing page (Accounts Overview).
test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected away from the Login page to the authenticated landing page (Accounts Overview) instead of seeing the login form.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Email/Username is required

test_cases.title: Register with valid details
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (First Name, Last Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, Phone Number, Social Security Number, Username, Password, Confirm Password) with valid values
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Account created successfully — please sign in and the user is redirected to the login page.

test_cases.title: Phone Number is automatically formatted as user types
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Enter unformatted digits into the Phone Number field
Observe the Phone Number field value
Click "Register" or move focus away to confirm formatting persists
test_cases.expected_result: Phone Number is automatically formatted to (123) 456-7890 as digits are entered and remains formatted on blur.

test_cases.title: Social Security Number is automatically formatted as user types
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Enter unformatted digits into the Social Security Number field
Observe the Social Security Number field value
Click "Register" or move focus away to confirm formatting persists
test_cases.expected_result: SSN is automatically formatted to 123-45-6789 as digits are entered and remains formatted on blur.

test_cases.title: Register with invalid SSN format
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except Social Security Number
Enter an SSN that does not match the required pattern into Social Security Number
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown for Social Security Number indicating incorrect format.
test_cases.spec_evidence: SSN must follow the format 123-45-6789 with automatic formatting

test_cases.title: Register with invalid ZIP Code format
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except ZIP Code
Enter a ZIP Code that does not match 5-digit or 5+4 formats into ZIP Code
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown for ZIP Code indicating incorrect format.
test_cases.spec_evidence: ZIP Code must be 5 digits or 5+4 format (12345 or 12345-6789)

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: On submission, the system validates field completeness, pattern conformance, and password match

test_cases.title: Register with invalid phone number format
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except Phone Number
Enter a phone number that does not conform to the required pattern into Phone Number
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown for Phone Number indicating incorrect format.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Phone Number must follow the format (123) 456-7890 with automatic formatting

test_cases.title: Register with non-matching Confirm Password
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except Password and Confirm Password
Enter a valid Password and a different value in Confirm Password
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating Confirm Password must match the Password field.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Confirm Password must match the Password field

test_cases.title: Register with invalid username (non-email format)
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except Username
Enter an invalid non-email value into Username
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Field-level error indicating the Username must be a valid email format.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Username must be a valid email format

test_cases.title: Register with short password below minimum length
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except Password and Confirm Password
Enter a Password shorter than the minimum length and repeat the same in Confirm Password
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the Password does not meet the minimum length requirement.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Password must be at least 8 characters

test_cases.title: Accounts table displays all customer accounts
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview page is open.
steps: Observe the accounts table on the page
Verify the table lists an entry for each account belonging to the user
test_cases.expected_result: The table displays all customer accounts.

test_cases.title: Dashboard shows welcome message with the user's name
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview page is open.
steps: Observe the welcome message area on the Accounts Overview page
Verify the welcome message contains the logged-in user's name
test_cases.expected_result: Welcome message displays the user's name.

test_cases.title: Footer row displays total balance equal to sum of account balances
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview page is open.
steps: Read the Current Balance value from each account row and compute their sum
Compare the computed sum to the footer row total balance displayed in the table
test_cases.expected_result: Footer row total balance equals the sum of the Current Balance values from all account rows.

test_cases.title: Each account row shows required columns (Account Number, Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status, Open Date)
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview page is open.
steps: Inspect each visible account row in the accounts table
Verify each row displays: Account Number, Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status (Active badge), and Open Date
test_cases.expected_result: Every account row contains the listed columns and the Account Status is displayed as an Active badge where applicable.

test_cases.title: Account numbers are masked showing only the last four digits
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview page is open.
steps: Inspect the Account Number value displayed in each account row
Verify the display masks all but the last four digits and follows the masked pattern
test_cases.expected_result: Account numbers are displayed in masked form, revealing only the last four digits.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Account numbers are masked for security (showing only last 4 digits as ****5001)

test_cases.title: Account rows are ordered by account creation date (earliest first)
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview page is open.
steps: Extract the Open Date value from each account row in the table order
Verify the sequence of Open Date values is ordered with the earliest date first
test_cases.expected_result: Account rows are listed in the table ordered by creation date with the earliest account first.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Rows are ordered by account creation date (earliest first)

test_cases.title: Account Number is clickable but does not navigate to details
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview page is open.
steps: Click the Account Number element in a visible account row
Observe whether the click causes navigation or opens an account detail view
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking the Account Number does not navigate to account details or open a detail view (clickable but not implemented yet).

test_cases.title: Open a new Savings account successfully
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and Open New Account page is open.
steps: Select the Savings account type card
Fill all required fields (Initial Deposit Amount meeting Savings minimum, Funding Source Account with sufficient balance)
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Displays the success message "Account opened successfully!", redirects to accounts overview, and the new account appears in the accounts listing.

test_cases.title: Reject opening when Initial Deposit is below Savings minimum
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and Open New Account page is open.
steps: Select the Savings account type card
Fill Initial Deposit Amount with an amount below the required Savings minimum and select a Funding Source Account with sufficient balance
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the initial deposit does not meet the required minimum is displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Initial Deposit Amount must meet minimums: $25 for Checking, $100 for Savings

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and Open New Account page is open.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validation error shown when account type is not selected

test_cases.title: Initial Deposit Amount non-numeric shows validation error
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and Open New Account page is open.
steps: Select an account type
Fill Initial Deposit Amount with non-numeric input and select a Funding Source Account with sufficient balance
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating Initial Deposit Amount must be numeric is displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Initial Deposit Amount must be numeric

test_cases.title: Real-time validation messages appear while entering invalid deposit
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and Open New Account page is open.
steps: Select an account type
Type non-numeric input into the Initial Deposit Amount field and observe validation message without submitting
test_cases.expected_result: Real-time validation error is displayed for the Initial Deposit Amount field.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Displays real-time validation errors

test_cases.title: Perform an internal transfer successfully
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and on the Transfer Funds page with at least two own accounts
steps: Select the internal transfer type (My ParaBank Account)
Fill all required fields (Source Account: select one own account, Destination Account: select a different own account, Transfer Amount: enter a valid amount within available balance)
Click "Transfer"
test_cases.expected_result: Confirmation message "Transfer completed successfully." is displayed and a transaction ID is shown.

test_cases.title: Complete an external transfer successfully
test_cases.preconditions: A source account exists with sufficient funds.
steps: Select transfer type 'External'
Fill all required fields (Source Account, External Account number, Confirm account number, Transfer Amount with a valid amount within available balance)
Click 'Submit'
test_cases.expected_result: Success message 'Transfer completed successfully.' is displayed and a transaction ID is shown.

test_cases.title: Reject transfer when funds are insufficient
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page and the selected Source Account has an insufficient balance for the requested transfer
steps: Select the internal transfer type (My ParaBank Account)
Fill all required fields (Source Account: select low-balance account, Destination Account: select another own account, Transfer Amount: enter amount greater than available balance)
Click "Transfer"
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer is blocked and a contextual error indicating insufficient funds is displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates sufficient funds

test_cases.title: Fail external transfer when account numbers do not match
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Select transfer type 'External'
Fill all required fields with mismatched Account number and Confirm account number
Click 'Submit'
test_cases.expected_result: Error displayed: 'Account numbers do not match.'
test_cases.spec_evidence: Account numbers do not match.

test_cases.title: Destination options update when transfer type is External
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Select transfer type 'External'
Observe the destination options presented on the form
test_cases.expected_result: Destination options update to include External Account fields (Account number and Confirm account number) appropriate for external transfers.

test_cases.title: Destination options update when transfer type set to internal
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page
steps: Change the Transfer type to the internal option (My ParaBank Account)
Open the Destination options and inspect available choices
test_cases.expected_result: Destination options reflect the internal transfer type by listing the user's own accounts as selectable destinations.

test_cases.title: Source Account dropdown shows only Checking and Savings accounts
test_cases.preconditions: User has multiple account types including Checking and Savings.
steps: Open the Source Account dropdown
Observe the list of accounts shown
test_cases.expected_result: Source Account dropdown is filtered to show only Checking and Savings accounts.

test_cases.title: Source Account dropdown lists only Checking and Savings for internal transfers
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page and has multiple account types
steps: Select the internal transfer type (My ParaBank Account)
Open the Source Account dropdown and inspect available options
test_cases.expected_result: Source Account dropdown contains only Checking and Savings account options.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Transfer"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates transfer amount

test_cases.title: Validation error for invalid transfer amount
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Select transfer type 'External'
Fill all required fields using an invalid Transfer Amount (for example negative or non-numeric)
Click 'Submit'
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the transfer amount is invalid.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates transfer amount

test_cases.title: Submit valid payment successfully and update balances
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and has a source account with sufficient funds.
steps: Fill all required fields (Payee Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, Phone Number, Payee Account Number, Confirm Account Number) with matching valid values
Fill Payment Amount with an amount less than or equal to the selected source account's available balance and select the Source Account
Click "Pay"
test_cases.expected_result: Payment executes; confirmation message "Payment submitted successfully." is shown with a reference code; source account balance is reduced by the payment amount.

test_cases.title: Recover from form error and resubmit payment successfully
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and has a source account that can cover the corrected payment amount.
steps: Fill all required fields but deliberately enter a value that triggers a validation error (e.g., non-matching account numbers or an over-limit payment amount)
Click "Pay" and observe the inline error while keeping the form editable
Correct the problematic field(s) to valid values, ensure the Payment Amount is within the source account balance, and click "Pay" again
test_cases.expected_result: After correction, payment executes; confirmation message "Payment submitted successfully." appears with a reference code and the source account balance is updated.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Pay"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields and the form remains editable.
test_cases.spec_evidence: System validates all entries

test_cases.title: Request a loan with valid inputs and sufficient collateral
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Select the appropriate loan type card (Personal, Auto, or Home)

Fill all required fields (Loan Amount within allowed range for selected type, Down Payment meeting the minimum percentage and less than the Loan Amount, select a Collateral Account with sufficient funds)
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Success message displayed "Loan approved and created successfully!" and the newly created loan is shown with its account details.

test_cases.title: Receive credit denial showing insufficient credit history
test_cases.preconditions: A test user profile that will be denied by the credit engine is available
steps: Select any loan type card
Fill all required fields with valid Loan Amount, Down Payment, and Collateral selection for that user
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Loan application denied and a specific reason such as "Insufficient credit history" is displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Insufficient credit history

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates amount ranges

test_cases.title: Reject loan when down payment is below the minimum percentage
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Select any loan type card
Fill all required fields with a valid Loan Amount and a Down Payment less than the minimum required percentage, and select a valid Collateral Account
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Loan application rejected with an error indicating the down payment does not meet the minimum percentage requirement.
test_cases.spec_evidence: 10% minimum down payment requirement

test_cases.title: Update profile with valid contact information
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Update Contact Info form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (First Name, Last Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, Phone Number) with valid values
Click "Update Profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile updated successfully and the form refreshes to show the updated data.

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid ZIP Code format
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Update Contact Info form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (First Name, Last Name, Street Address, City, State, Phone Number) with valid values and enter an invalid format in ZIP Code
Click "Update Profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown for ZIP Code, the ZIP field is highlighted, and an inline error banner is displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Submission triggers validation of each entry (format and completeness)

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Update Contact Info form is open.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Update Profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields; invalid fields are highlighted and an inline error banner is displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Submission triggers validation of each entry (format and completeness)

test_cases.title: Request a Debit card with valid account and complete address
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the card request form is visible.
steps: Select Card Type as Debit, choose a valid account to link, and fill a complete shipping address
Click "Request Card"
test_cases.expected_result: Confirmation shown stating card request submitted successfully with a tracking ID and a card-request ticket is opened.

test_cases.title: Update card controls with valid limit, travel notice, and status change
test_cases.preconditions: User has at least one Active card.
steps: Select an existing card from the "Select Existing Card" dropdown
Fill all required fields (New Spending Limit with a valid amount, Travel Notice with valid start/end dates and destinations, Card Status set to an allowable target state)
Click "Update Controls"
test_cases.expected_result: Shows "Card controls updated successfully." and the card's status and spending limit reflect the updates.

test_cases.title: Disallow invalid card-status transition
test_cases.preconditions: A card exists in a status that disallows the attempted transition.
steps: Select that existing card from the "Select Existing Card" dropdown
Set Card Status to the disallowed target state (keep other fields valid)
Click "Update Controls"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates the status transition is not allowed and the form remains editable.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates allowable card-status transitions

test_cases.title: Submit when selected account is not in good standing
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the card request form is visible and a selected account is not in good standing.
steps: Select Card Type, choose an account that is not in good standing, and fill a complete shipping address
Click "Request Card"
test_cases.expected_result: Request is blocked with an error indicating the account standing prevents card issuance; no ticket is created.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system verifies the selected account’s standing

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the card request form is visible.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Request Card"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system checks that the address is complete

test_cases.title: Submit with incomplete shipping address
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the card request form is visible.
steps: Select Card Type
Select an Account to Link
Fill an incomplete Shipping Address
Click "Request Card"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the address is incomplete and no card-request ticket is opened.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system checks that the address is complete

test_cases.title: Reject New Spending Limit above allowed policy
test_cases.preconditions: At least one card exists.
steps: Select an existing card from the "Select Existing Card" dropdown
Fill New Spending Limit with an amount above the allowed policy (keep other fields valid)
Click "Update Controls"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates the spending limit exceeds policy and the form remains editable.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates numeric limits

test_cases.title: Reject Travel Notice with end date before start date
test_cases.preconditions: At least one card exists.
steps: Select an existing card from the "Select Existing Card" dropdown
Fill Travel Notice with an end date earlier than the start date and provide destinations
Click "Update Controls"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates an invalid travel date range and the form remains editable.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates date ranges

test_cases.title: Card Type dropdown lists Debit and Credit options
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the card request form is visible.
steps: Open the Card Type dropdown
Verify the dropdown options include Debit and Credit
test_cases.expected_result: Card Type dropdown contains Debit and Credit options.

test_cases.title: Create recurring investment plan with weekly frequency
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the Create Plan form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (Fund Symbol, Contribution Amount, Frequency set to Weekly, Start Date set to a valid future date, Funding Account with sufficient balance)
Click "Create Plan"
test_cases.expected_result: Schedule is stored and the success message "Plan created successfully." is displayed.

test_cases.title: Sell fund with valid symbol and sufficient shares executes same-day trade
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Execute Trade form is open; selected account holds at least the requested shares.
steps: Fill all required fields (Action: Sell, Fund Symbol: valid existing symbol via autocomplete, Quantity: positive number within share balance, Destination Account: account holding the shares)
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Trade executed successfully. A same-day trade is completed, holdings are updated, and a confirmation displays with an order ID.

test_cases.title: Submit with non-existent fund symbol
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Execute Trade form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields with a fund symbol that does not exist (use the Fund Symbol autocomplete to enter a non-existent symbol)
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates the fund symbol is invalid or not found and the trade is not executed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system checks that the symbol exists

test_cases.title: Submit with contribution amount below minimum
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the Create Plan form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except set Contribution Amount below the required minimum
Click "Create Plan"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown for Contribution Amount and the field is highlighted indicating it must meet the minimum.
test_cases.spec_evidence: the contribution meets the minimum

test_cases.title: Buy order rejected when buying power is insufficient
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Execute Trade form is open; selected funding account balance is insufficient for the requested buy.
steps: Fill all required fields (Action: Buy, Fund Symbol: valid existing symbol, Quantity: amount requiring more buying power than available in the selected funding account)
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates insufficient buying power and the trade is not executed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: the customer has sufficient buying power or share balance

test_cases.title: Submit with funding account lacking adequate balance
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the Create Plan form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except choose a Funding Account that does not have enough balance for the Contribution Amount
Click "Create Plan"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown indicating insufficient funds and the Funding Account field is highlighted.
test_cases.spec_evidence: the funding account has adequate balance

test_cases.title: Sell order rejected when share balance is insufficient
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Execute Trade form is open; selected account holds fewer shares than the requested sell quantity.
steps: Fill all required fields (Action: Sell, Fund Symbol: valid existing symbol, Quantity: greater than current share balance in the selected account)
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates insufficient share balance and the trade is not executed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: the customer has sufficient buying power or share balance

test_cases.title: Submit with quantity equal to zero
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Execute Trade form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields with Quantity set to zero
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates quantity must be greater than zero and the trade is not executed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: quantity is greater than zero

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Execute Trade form is open.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown inline for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: validation failures appear inline

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the Create Plan form is open.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Create Plan"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields and problematic fields are highlighted.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Errors highlight the problematic fields

test_cases.title: Submit with start date not in the future
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the Create Plan form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except set Start Date to today or a past date
Click "Create Plan"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown and Start Date field is highlighted indicating the date must be in the future.
test_cases.spec_evidence: the start date is in the future

test_cases.title: Generate statement using custom date range
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and Account Statements page is open.
steps: Select an account from the Account dropdown and set a valid custom Start Date and End Date in Statement Period
Click "Generate Statement"
test_cases.expected_result: Statement generated successfully. Transactions for the selected account and date range are displayed.

test_cases.title: Save e-statement preference with valid email (opt into paperless)
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and on the Account Statements page.
steps: Check the paperless statements checkbox to opt into e-statements
Fill Email Address with a valid email address
Click "Save Preference"
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "e-Statement preference updated." and the preference and email are saved.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and Account Statements page is open.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Generate Statement"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates the dates

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: Paperless checkbox selected
steps: Ensure the checkbox to opt into paperless statements is selected and leave the Email Address field empty
Click "Save Preference"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown for Email Address; email field highlighted with guidance and no success message displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Failure highlights the email field with guidance

test_cases.title: Handle statement generation failure from backend
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and Account Statements page is open.
steps: Select an account and a valid statement period (month-or custom date range)
Click "Generate Statement"
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "Unable to generate statement — please try again later." and no statement is produced.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Unable to generate statement — please try again later.

test_cases.title: Change password with valid current and matching strong new password
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and Change Password panel is visible.
steps: Fill all required fields (Current Password, New Password, Confirm New Password) with valid current password and a strong matching new password
Click "Change Password"
test_cases.expected_result: Credentials updated and "Password changed successfully." message displayed.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and Change Password panel is visible.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Change Password"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validation errors highlight the appropriate fields.

test_cases.title: Submit callback request with valid inputs
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and Request Callback form is displayed.
steps: Fill all required fields (Reason for Call, Preferred Date set to a valid date at least the next business day, Preferred Time Window, Phone Number with a valid format or keep pre-filled)
Click "Request Callback"
test_cases.expected_result: Success message "Callback request submitted." is displayed and an email confirmation is sent.

test_cases.title: Send support message successfully without attachment
test_cases.preconditions: Support Center 'Send Message' form is open
steps: Fill all required fields (Subject, Category, Message Body) with valid inputs
Click "Send Message"
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "Message sent successfully." and shows a ticket ID.

test_cases.title: Edit pre-filled phone number and submit callback request
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and Request Callback form is displayed with Phone Number pre-filled.
steps: Update the Phone Number field with a valid phone number format
Fill remaining required fields (Reason for Call, Preferred Date at least the next business day, Preferred Time Window)
Click "Request Callback"
test_cases.expected_result: Success message "Callback request submitted." is displayed, an email confirmation is sent, and the updated phone number is used for the request.

test_cases.title: Send message with message body empty
test_cases.preconditions: Support Center 'Send Message' form is open
steps: Fill all other required fields, leave Message Body empty
Click "Send Message"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation indicating message body is required.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system ensures a message body is present

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and Request Callback form is displayed.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Request Callback"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown inline for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validation errors are shown inline

test_cases.title: Send message with subject exceeding maximum length
test_cases.preconditions: Support Center 'Send Message' form is open
steps: Fill all required fields with valid inputs except enter an overly long value in Subject
Click "Send Message"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation indicating subject length is invalid.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system checks subject length

test_cases.title: Submit with Preferred Date set to next business day
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and Request Callback form is displayed.
steps: Fill all required fields with Preferred Date set exactly to the next business day and other fields valid
Click "Request Callback"
test_cases.expected_result: Success message "Callback request submitted." is displayed and an email confirmation is sent.
test_cases.spec_evidence: date is at least the next business day
